content.page_title: Best Bariatric Surgeons in Regina, Saskatchewan (2026)
content.meta_description: Find top-rated bariatric surgeons in Regina, SK. Expert weight loss surgery options, costs, and insurance coverage for Saskatchewan residents.
content.h1: Best Bariatric Surgeons in Regina, Saskatchewan
content.intro: If you're considering bariatric surgery in Regina, you're taking a serious step toward lasting weight loss and better health. This guide walks you through the best bariatric surgeons and surgical weight loss programs available to Saskatchewan residents, including what's covered by AHCIP and what you can expect to pay out of pocket.
content.local_context: Regina residents face obesity rates slightly above the Canadian average, with approximately 28% of the population classified as obese. The city's main healthcare hub, Regina General Hospital, offers some bariatric services, though many residents also travel to Saskatoon or beyond for specialized sur

top_picks.name: Regina Bariatric Surgery Centre
top_picks.tagline: Comprehensive surgical and non-surgical weight loss programs
top_picks.description: Regina's leading dedicated bariatric facility offering gastric bypass, gastric sleeve, and lap-band procedures. Multi-disciplinary team includes surgeons, nutritionists, and mental health professionals. Accepts AHCIP referrals and offers pre- and post-operative support groups.
top_picks.price_range: AHCIP covered with referral; private consultations $200-400
cons: Waitlist can be 6-12 months for AHCIP patients
top_picks.best_for: Patients seeking comprehensive, locally-based surgical weight loss with strong follow-up care

top_picks.name: Prairie Weight Loss Surgery (Affiliated with Regina General)
top_picks.tagline: Hospital-integrated bariatric program with proven outcomes
top_picks.description: Partnership with Regina General Hospital offering minimally invasive bariatric surgery. Surgeons are fellowship-trained in bariatric procedures. Program includes mandatory pre-operative education and 2-year post-operative follow-up. AHCIP coverage available for eligible candidates.
top_picks.price_range: AHCIP fully covered; private surgery $8,000-15,000 CAD
cons: Longer waitlists due to public system demand
Less personalized attention in large program
top_picks.best_for: Patients who prefer hospital-integrated care and qualify for AHCIP coverage

top_picks.name: Wharton Medical Clinic - Regina
top_picks.tagline: Medical weight loss and pre-surgical preparation
top_picks.description: National chain with Regina location specializing in medically-supervised weight loss programs, including GLP-1 medications (Ozempic, Saxenda) and comprehensive pre-surgical assessments. Helps patients prepare for or explore alternatives to bariatric surgery.
cons: May not suit candidates needing surgery
top_picks.best_for: Patients exploring medical weight loss first or seeking pre-operative conditioning

top_picks.name: Saskatoon Bariatric Institute (Referral Partner)
top_picks.tagline: Specialized bariatric centre 2 hours away with advanced techniques
top_picks.description: While based in Saskatoon, this centre accepts AHCIP referrals from Regina residents and offers advanced bariatric techniques including robotic-assisted surgery. Many Regina patients travel for surgery and return for follow-up locally. Strong reputation across Saskatchewan.
cons: Travel required for surgery and early follow-ups
top_picks.best_for: Regina patients seeking cutting-edge surgical techniques and willing to travel

top_picks.name: Herbal Magic - Regina
top_picks.description: Franchise-based clinic offering meal replacement programs, herbal supplements, and lifestyle coaching. Non-medical approach suitable for patients exploring alternatives to surgery or seeking post-operative nutritional support. Multiple Regina locations.
top_picks.best_for: Patients seeking gentle, non-surgical weight management with local support

top_picks.name: Regina Family Medicine - Bariatric Clinic
top_picks.tagline: Primary care-based bariatric assessment and referral
top_picks.description: Clinic within Regina Family Medicine offering initial bariatric assessments, AHCIP referral coordination, and post-operative primary care. Ideal entry point for patients unsure about surgery or needing local medical oversight during the decision process.
top_picks.price_range: Covered by AHCIP; consultations included in provincial coverage
cons: May require referral to surgeon anyway
top_picks.best_for: Patients starting their bariatric journey and needing coordinated primary care

what_to_look_for: Board certification in bariatric surgery and fellowship training from recognized programs
Clear explanation of AHCIP coverage, waitlists, and what out-of-pocket costs may apply
Comprehensive pre-operative assessment including nutritional, psychological, and medical evaluation
Structured post-operative follow-up program lasting at least 2 years with support groups and nutritionist access
Transparent discussion of risks, benefits, and realistic outcomes for your specific procedure
Experience with revision surgery in case you need adjustments or corrections
content.average_cost_context: In Saskatchewan, bariatric surgery covered by AHCIP is free for eligible patients; private surgery in Regina ranges from $8,000–$15,000 CAD depending on procedure type. Medical weight loss programs average $150–$300/month and are not covered by provincial insurance.
content.insurance_notes: Alberta Health Services (AHCIP) covers bariatric surgery for eligible Saskatchewan residents who meet clinical criteria: BMI ≥40 or BMI ≥35 with obesity-related comorbidities, documented weight loss attempts, and psychological clearance. Referral from a physician is required. Waitlists typically range from 6–18 months. Medications like GLP-1 agonists are not routinely covered by AHCIP but may be partially covered through private benefits.

faqs.question: How long is the waitlist for bariatric surgery in Regina through AHCIP?
faqs.answer: AHCIP waitlists for bariatric surgery in Regina typically range from 6 to 18 months, depending on the procedure and surgeon availability. Some private clinics offer shorter timelines. Contact Regina Bariatric Surgery Centre or Prairie Weight Loss Surgery for current estimates.

faqs.question: Do I need a referral to see a bariatric surgeon in Regina?
faqs.answer: For AHCIP coverage, yes—you need a referral from your family physician or primary care provider. Private consultations may not require a referral. Start by speaking with your doctor about whether bariatric surgery is appropriate for you.

faqs.question: What's the difference between gastric bypass and gastric sleeve surgery?
faqs.answer: Gastric bypass reduces stomach size and reroutes the intestines, limiting both calorie intake and absorption. Gastric sleeve reduces stomach size but keeps normal intestinal function. Bypass typically results in more weight loss but carries slightly higher risks. Your surgeon will recommend the best option for your health profile.

faqs.question: How much weight can I expect to lose after bariatric surgery?
faqs.answer: Weight loss varies by procedure and individual. Gastric bypass patients typically lose 60–80% of excess weight within 18–24 months. Gastric sleeve patients lose 50–70% of excess weight. Success depends on diet, exercise, and lifestyle changes post-surgery.

faqs.question: Are there any risks or side effects after bariatric surgery?
faqs.answer: Common side effects include nausea, vomiting, and nutrient deficiencies (requiring lifelong vitamin supplementation). Rare serious complications include blood clots, leaks, and bowel obstruction. Your surgical team will discuss all risks before surgery and monitor you closely afterward.

faqs.question: Can I eat normally after bariatric surgery?
faqs.answer: No. After surgery, you'll eat much smaller portions (typically 1–2 cups per meal) and must avoid high-calorie, sugary, and fatty foods. Most patients adapt within 3–6 months. Nutritionist guidance is essential for long-term success.

faqs.question: Will my family doctor in Regina follow me after surgery?
faqs.answer: Yes. Your family physician can provide routine primary care post-surgery. However, your bariatric surgeon's team (including nutritionists and specialists) will manage surgery-specific follow-up. Coordination between your GP and surgical team ensures seamless care.

content.cta_heading: Ready to explore bariatric surgery in Regina?
content.cta_body: Start by booking a consultation with your family physician to discuss whether bariatric surgery is right for you.
